how do we use the backend api? Let's create a doc for developers and a doc for ai agents because we ideally want AI Agents to be first class citizens and have a fully documented API and an MCP.
push to git

Check the console log. Sending an empty body for /search/geo-grid causes a 500 error, we need to return a usefull message with the error code to the caller. Sending a JSON body as described in the API-DEVELOPER-DOCUMENTATION.md file in the BASIC USAGE section: 2. Basic Usage
// Submit a geographic grid search
const response = await fetch('http://localhost:3232/search/geo-grid', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    origin: { lat: 40.7128, lng: -74.0060 }, // New York City
    query: 'coffee shops',
    shape: 'square', // 'square' or 'circle'
    distance: 1,     // 1 mile
    distanceUnit: 'mi',
    size: 3          // 3x3 grid (9 total points)
  })
});

const { jobId } = await response.json();
console.log('Job submitted:', jobId);

// Check job status
const statusResponse = await fetch(`http://localhost:3232/search/geo-grid/${jobId}`);
const job = await statusResponse.json();
console.log('Job status:', job.status);


This returns a 201 status code and the following JSON body: {
    "status": "pending",
    "message": "Job accepted"
}

but we don't have a job number to follow up with, and the server throws an error:

We are not extracting the data properly for the business. the google place id (should be the value that goes into the business table in the field 'place_id' is found in the 'place_link' in the query parameter 'data' it is after the last '!' band before the '?' or the end of the url. It always starts with 'Ch'. use this example to see for yourself where the url contains the place_id of 'ChIJv6INkhtZwokR5Ol7iuc6TJc': https://www.google.com/maps/place/Urban+Backyard/data=!4m7!3m6!1s0x89c2591b920da2bf:0x974c3ae78a7be9e4!8m2!3d40.7208064!4d-73.9966015!16s%2Fg%2F11g0tpkwp1!19sChIJv6INkhtZwokR5Ol7iuc6TJc?authuser=0&hl=en&rclk=1
